title: Performance Review Process and Self-Assessment Tips
text:
Q: What is the performance review process, timeline, and how can I effectively complete my self-assessment?
A: The performance review process occurs annually, typically starting in January and concluding by the end of March. Employees will receive a notification in early January to begin their self-assessment, which is due by January 31. During this period, you should reflect on your accomplishments, challenges, and areas for improvement over the past year. After self-assessments are submitted, managers will review them and schedule one-on-one meetings to discuss performance feedback and set goals for the upcoming year. These meetings usually take place in February. To effectively complete your self-assessment, focus on specific examples of your work, align your achievements with company goals, and be honest about areas where you can grow. If you have questions or need assistance, please reach out to your HR representative or your direct supervisor for guidance.
